title: Nested Object Transformation
description: Write a function that transforms a nested object of users with their scores into a sorted array of user summaries. This exercise teaches you how to work with complex nested data structures.
instructions: Create a function named `transformUsers` that accepts an object where keys are user IDs and values are user objects
Each user object has: name (string), scores (array of numbers), and active (boolean)
Calculate the average score for each user
Return an array of objects with: id, name, averageScore, and isActive
Sort the result by averageScore in descending order
Only include active users in the result

hints.content: Use Object.entries() to convert the object into an array of [id, user] pairs. Then you can use array methods to filter, map, and sort.
Chain the operations: filter for active users, map to create new objects with calculated averageScore, then sort by averageScore in descending order (b.averageScore - a.averageScore).
Calculate average with: user.scores.reduce((sum, score) => sum + score, 0) / user.scores.length. Remember to filter active users first, then transform and sort.
